created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — fresh v3.1 build, supersedes v2.0 record

ukhab_identity.secondary_codes_notes: None by default. A specific ley may carry a management/condition secondary code in survey; not applied at type level.

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (the statutory classification underpinning the Biodiversity Metric)
sources.specific_claim: c1b is the UKHab Level 4 code under c1 Arable and horticulture (c Cropland). Canonical Level 4 name 'Temporary grass and clover leys'. Sibling codes c1c Cereal crops, c1d Non-cereal crops, c1f Horticulture.

sources.title: A358 Taunton to Southfields — Ecological Baseline Report (UK Habitat Classification)
sources.specific_claim: Independent confirmation that UKHab c1b = 'Temporary grass and clover leys' (used in an applied baseline survey alongside c1c/c1d/c1f).

nvc_communities: MG7 (agricultural analogue — Lolium perenne leys / rye-grass leys)
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: Sown agricultural leys sit outside true semi-natural NVC; MG7 (Lolium perenne leys) is the conventional NVC analogue rather than a classified community of the parcel. No constancy table applied at type level.
cross_references.bng_metric_habitat_name: Cropland — Temporary grass and clover leys
cross_references.wild_sheet_ref_note: Sheet-less by design: a Regenerate-zone production sward with no semi-natural field sheet. Field guidance comes from the grazing tools, not a habitat sheet.

sources.title: British Plant Communities, Vol. 3 — Grasslands and montane communities (MG7 rye-grass leys)
sources.specific_claim: MG7 is the NVC unit for Lolium perenne leys; used here as analogue only. Standard reference, not independently re-verified this session.

sources.title: Handbook for Phase 1 Habitat Survey (B4 Improved grassland)
sources.specific_claim: Improved/agricultural grassland incl. leys maps to Phase 1 B4. Standard reference, not independently re-verified this session.

ecology.description: An intensively managed, sown grassland dominated by perennial ryegrass (Lolium perenne) with white clover (Trifolium repens), and in multi-species/herbal variants red clover (Trifolium pratense) and forbs. Established as a short- to medium-term rotational ley (typically 1–5 years) for high dry-matter production and as a soil-fertility/break phase in arable and mixed systems.
condition_indicators_good: Perennial ryegrass dominant (>60–70% cover), dense and leafy
White clover well established and visible throughout (target ~25–35% DM contribution in grazing season)
Even, dense sward with high leaf:stem ratio and good ground cover
Minimal bare ground, docks, thistles or weed-grass ingress
condition_indicators_poor: Annual meadow-grass (Poa annua) >20% cover — low yield, low quality
Yorkshire fog (Holcus lanatus) / creeping bent (Agrostis stolonifera) ingress — low palatability
Clover declined or absent — loss of nitrogen fixation and protein
Open sward, poaching, docks (Rumex spp.) or creeping thistle (Cirsium arvense) establishing
ecosystem_functions: Very high dry-matter production — the system's primary forage engine
Biological nitrogen fixation via white/red clover (commonly cited ~150–280 kg N/ha/yr under good clover content)
Soil-fertility and structure building as an arable break phase (root mass, organic-matter return)
Rapid ground cover reducing erosion between arable phases
ecology.whole_system_role: Regenerate-zone production sward: the high-output forage base of the grazing system and a soil-regenerating break in the arable rotation. Productive value is high; ecological value is low by design and is managed around, not sacrificed for yield.

successional_neighbours.note: Weed-grass ingress and clover loss drift a neglected ley toward modified/semi-improved permanent grassland (g4).
Reseeded from, and ploughed back into, the arable phase of the rotation.

sources.title: Clover species for grassland reseeding
sources.specific_claim: White clover fixes nitrogen for the companion grass; well-managed clover content supports substantial biological N fixation in grazing leys.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel in survey, not at type level. The 1–5 descriptors below define the production-criteria scale for c1b.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Excellent / optimal
condition_grade_descriptors.description: PRG-dominant, dense, leafy; clover ~25–35% DM; high D-value; negligible weed grasses.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Good
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Productive but with minor weed-grass ingress and/or some clover decline.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Moderate
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Notable weed-grass ingress, reduced clover; yield and quality clearly below potential.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Poor
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Weed grasses dominant, clover sparse/absent, open sward; low yield and quality.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Degraded / reclassify
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Sward reverted to semi-improved character; reclassify (commonly to g4 modified grassland).

sources.title: Check swards for forage potential (NIAB figures cited)
sources.specific_claim: Sward composition (PRG vs weed grasses) drives forage potential; weed grasses yield ~7–28% of PRG potential — basis for the production grade descriptors.

abiotic_template.parent_material_geology: Variable — sown on any cultivable soil; not geology-determined
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Typically limed and fertilised to near-neutral pH (~6.0–6.5) to favour clover; a managed rather than a site-determined template.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: REGENERATE TARGET STATE (per Schema v3.1 convention): these dials describe the productive target the layer aims for — the 'productive peak of the humped-back curve' — not a current measured state.

water.descriptor: Target: aggregated soil holding moisture within its structure without waterlogging; free drainage with good plant-available water.

earth.descriptor: Target: well-structured, aggregated, biologically active fertile topsoil; aerobic mineral cycling.

air.descriptor: Target: well-aerated soil supporting root respiration and clover nitrogen fixation.

fire.descriptor: Target: high photosynthesis and leaf area; a fast-running mineral cycle and high net primary production.

successional_envelope.current_stage: Sown productive sward held at an early, high-production stage (arrested succession)
range_of_natural_variability: Productive PRG/clover ley maintained by grazing/cutting and periodic reseed
If under-managed: weed-grass ingress toward semi-improved/modified grassland (g4)
If abandoned: coarse tussocky grassland and scrub colonisation over time
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: Held at the productive stage by supplied disturbance and reseeding. Without management the fork runs toward semi-improved grassland (g4); it does not self-maintain as a productive ley.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: Very high net primary production — the system's primary production engine. Large share of the harvestable energy budget with rapid throughput, but feeds a narrow, simplified food web.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: A production sward whose purpose is high forage output while rebuilding soil function and fertility. It is treated to lift soil biology, clover content and utilisation — the defining Regenerate aim — rather than protected or handed back to natural process.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Supplied — grazing/cutting and periodic reseed; the manager holds the dial throughout.

wild_management_target.detail: Maintain the productive target state: PRG/clover balance (clover ~25–35% DM), high utilisation via rotational/paddock grazing, building soil organic matter and structure over the ley phase. Re-grade trigger: two seasons measured a band above/below current grade → re-grade (the grazing plan is the measuring instrument).

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: Low ecological value — near-monoculture with low botanical and invertebrate diversity and no specialist habitat. Per the 'two values' principle this is honestly low ecologically and high productively; design around it rather than sacrifice other habitats for its yield.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Ubiquitous and fully replaceable; re-sown at will.

diversity.notes: Very low botanical and structural diversity.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: High primary productivity but low trophic complexity and transfer; energy is largely harvested, not cycled through a diverse food web.

wild_assessment.naturalistic_lu_ha_note: Not applicable — Bioregionally Wilder field only. c1b is a Regenerate production sward.
wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: Not applicable — a managed production sward, not a component patch of a wilder grazing mosaic.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: False in context. (In a hypothetical wilder mosaic a ley-like green oasis would be grazed first; here it is a managed sward, not a mosaic patch.)
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Empty by design — Regenerate uses a state target, not process indicators.

sources.title: WILD Method Statement — The Science Behind WILD (elemental conditions, zones, Regenerate target-state convention)
sources.specific_claim: Framework basis for the elemental dials, zone assignment and the Regenerate target-state reading. Overlay/interpretive layer (not L1–L3 core data); recorded transparently as framework-derived.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Highly palatable to all ruminant classes; leafy PRG with clover is a preferred forage. Bloat risk on high-clover swards for naive/hungry stock — manage introduction.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: GROSS production, well-managed farm-scale PRG/WC ley (years 1–3). Below trial maxima of 16–18 t DM/ha (newly sown + high N, not farm-representative). Utilisable ≈ gross × utilisation_factor (~0.80 at grade 1) ≈ 8.8–11.2 t, consistent with AHDB's ~10 t commercial utilisation target.
Estimated from NIAB year-3 decline trajectory and weed-grass ingress (~20–30% reduction on grade 1).
Estimated — not directly sourced. Significant weed-grass ingress and clover decline.
Estimated — not directly sourced. Weed-grass dominant, sparse clover.

forage_yield_literature.notes: GROSS DM/ha/yr. Commercial farm-scale gross ~10–14 t for a well-managed ley; trial/optimal (RGCL, newly sown, high N, cutting) 16–18 t — not farm-representative. The ~10 t figure widely quoted for commercial systems is UTILISED, not gross; do not conflate (see dm_basis and utilisation_factor).

sources.title: Recommended Grass and Clover Lists for England and Wales 2025/26
sources.specific_claim: Top PRG/clover varieties exceed 11 t DM/ha in trials; trial conditions 16–18 t DM/ha under cutting with high N.

sources.title: Grass leys for the arable rotation
sources.specific_claim: RGCL trial yields 16–18 t DM/ha (newly sown, high N); commercial grazing target ~10 t DM/ha utilised; utilisation varies 50–80%.

utilisation_factor.notes: AHDB: set-stocked utilisation often <50% (≈6 t DM/ha realised); rotational 60–70%; well-managed paddock/cell grazing 75–80%. Regenerate target assumes rotational/paddock management. First-pass defaults — ground-truth per farm (Update Note: start low and measure up).

sources.title: Utilisation is key to a successful grazing system; Grazing cover targets for rotational grazing
sources.specific_claim: Graze to residual ~1,500 kg DM/ha (4 cm) from pre-graze 2,500–3,000 kg DM/ha; high utilisation from correct covers/residuals.

sources.title: Pasture management (AHDB 2018 utilisation figures cited)
sources.specific_claim: Set-stocked utilisation often below 50% with ~6 t DM/ha average annual yield; rotational sheep systems 60–65% utilisation.

forage.conservation_lu_ha_note: Not applicable — Protect & Restore field. For c1b, LU/ha is derived from gross DM × utilisation_factor ÷ 5.5 t/LU, not held as a direct conservation density.

nutritional_quality_by_grade.notes: Fresh leafy PRG in active growth. AHDB GrassCheckGB: spring peak ~12.1–12.2 MJ/kg DM, summer dip toward 11.5, autumn recovery ~12. CP peaks ~24% March, ~21% July, ~24% November. Clover lifts overall CP.
Estimated decline with weed-grass ingress and reduced clover.

nutritional_quality_sources.title: Forage for Knowledge end of year review 2023 (GrassCheckGB)
nutritional_quality_sources.specific_claim: Seasonal ME: spring peak 12.1–12.2 MJ/kg DM; summer <12; autumn recovery to 12. CP: March 24.3%, July 21%, November 24.4%.

nutritional_quality_sources.title: The Nutritional Value of Grass
nutritional_quality_sources.specific_claim: Leafy ryegrass 16–25% CP in DM; top varieties 75–80 D-value; 1 D-value point = 0.16 MJ/kg ME.

seasonal_forage_curve.summer: Moderate — growth and quality dip if sward stems; manage residuals
seasonal_forage_curve.autumn: Moderate–high — recovery flush in quality and growth
seasonal_forage_curve.winter: Low — minimal growth; deferred grazing only

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Well-matched to high-output sward; breed-level matching via the Animal entity.
Lambs and ewes utilise leafy ley efficiently.

unsuitable_animals.breed: Very hardy upland-only breeds (as a sole use)
unsuitable_animals.reason: Not unsuitable on welfare grounds, but high-output ground is wasted on stock selected for marginal forage; better deployed on semi-natural habitat.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Rotational grazing April–October; conservation cuts (silage) in the growing season
timing_windows.rationale: Match grazing to the growth curve; take surplus as silage to maintain quality through flushes
timing_windows.avoid: Winter poaching and over-grazing of clover stolons (causes clover death)
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Introduce naive/hungry stock to high-clover swards gradually (bloat risk).

management.intensity_guidance: Stocking is SCHEME-TIED and never averaged across schemes. Derive LU/ha from utilised DM ÷ 5.5 t per LU: at grade 1, gross 11–14 t × ~0.80 utilisation ≈ 8.8–11.2 t utilised ≈ ~1.6–2.0 LU/ha as a planning figure. England: express in GLU per the Natural England/ELM scheme definition; Scotland: express in LU per SAC/FAS TN686. Take each scheme's figure from its own definition — do not average GLU and LU.
management.monitoring_protocol: Plate meter / sward stick (pre-graze 2,500–3,000 kg DM/ha; residual ~1,500 kg DM/ha or 4 cm, AHDB); track clover %; apply the re-grade trigger (two seasons measured a band above/below → re-grade). The grazing plan is the measuring instrument.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Low distinctiveness in the Statutory Biodiversity Metric (Cropland — temporary leys). Minimal BNG unit value; significance is as a transition/management state, not a BNG asset.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Cropland/temporary leys carry low distinctiveness. Exact current metric label to be confirmed against the live Statutory Biodiversity Metric.

target_species_composition: Minimal weed grasses and broadleaf weeds
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full species records and the Habitat↔Species join (indicator role, constancy) are built in Phase 2B via the NVC bridge. c1b is sown, so the join is agronomic rather than NVC-constancy based.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (code and Level 4 name verified against UKHab v2.01 this session — name corrected to 'Temporary grass and clover leys')
forage grade-1 yield (gross) and nutritional quality (L2: RGCL 2025/26, AHDB, GrassCheckGB)
fields_partially_populated: cross_references — NVC (MG7 analogue), Phase 1 (B4), BNG label from standard references; not independently re-verified this session (medium confidence)
forage grades 2–5 and nutritional grades 2–5 — grade 2 reasoned from sourced decline data; grades 3–5 ESTIMATED, not directly sourced
wild_assessment.elemental_conditions — Regenerate TARGET-STATE dials, framework-reasoned (not measured); transparent per the v3.1 convention
management.intensity_guidance — LU/ha derivation method given; scheme-specific GLU/LU figures to be drawn from each scheme's definition
fields_pending_research: Exact current Statutory Biodiversity Metric label and distinctiveness band for c1b
Independent verification of MG7 / Phase 1 B4 / BNG crosswalk
Mineral and anti-nutrient data (Plant Species entity, Phase 2B)
Breed-level animal matching (Animal entity verification phase)
Per-farm ground-truthing of utilisation and derived LU/ha
data_completeness.notes: Core identity and grade-1 forage/nutrition are well-sourced to L1–L3; the WILD overlay is framework-reasoned and labelled as such; grades 3–5 are honest estimates flagged as such — never fabricated. Gross-vs-utilised DM is now cleanly separated (dm_basis: gross + utilisation_factor) so the grazing tools cannot double-count. This record is the v3.1 template for the remaining production swards (c1b7, g4) and Phase 2A.

sources.title: Grass leys for the arable rotation
Utilisation is key to a successful grazing system
